# Timeline of 2010

A chronological index of notable events in the year 2010, drawn from the knowledge base.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: timeline — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->

| Date | Event | Category | Link |
|------|-------|----------|------|
| January 4, 2010 | Burj Khalifa opens — Dubai inaugurates the world's tallest building at 828 metres, surpassing Taipei 101 and redefining the limits of skyscraper engineering | Science & Technology | [details](science-technology/burj-khalifa.md) |
| January 12, 2010 | Haiti earthquake — magnitude 7.0 quake near Port-au-Prince kills an estimated 100,000–316,000 people | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/haiti-earthquake.md) |
| January 12, 2010 | Google announces withdrawal from China — Google announces it will stop censoring search results in China, redirecting to Hong Kong servers | Science & Technology | [details](science-technology/google-china-withdrawal.md) |
| January 25, 2010 | *Avatar* becomes highest-grossing film — James Cameron's 3D epic surpasses *Titanic* to become the highest-grossing film of all time worldwide, driving the modern 3D cinema boom | Arts & Culture | [details](arts-culture/avatar.md) |
| Throughout 2010 | 3D cinema revolution — *Avatar*'s success catalyzes rapid 3D adoption across theaters and studios; 3D films earn about $1.3 billion in 2010 and establish durable premium theatrical pricing | Arts & Culture | [details](arts-culture/3d-cinema-revolution.md) |
| February 12–28, 2010 | 2010 Winter Olympics (Vancouver) — Canada hosts the Winter Games and wins a record 14 golds as host nation; Sidney Crosby's overtime goal seals men's hockey gold before 114 million viewers worldwide | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/2010-winter-olympics-vancouver.md) |
| February 27, 2010 | 2010 Chile earthquake — magnitude 8.8 megathrust quake and Pacific tsunami off the Maule coast kill 525 people; one of the strongest earthquakes ever instrumentally recorded | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/2010-chile-earthquake.md) |
| March 23, 2010 | Affordable Care Act signed — President Obama signs the most significant US healthcare overhaul since Medicare/Medicaid in 1965 | Society & Economics | [details](society-economics/affordable-care-act.md) |
| April 3, 2010 | iPad launched — Apple releases the iPad, establishing the modern tablet computing category and selling 15 million units in its first year | Science & Technology | [details](science-technology/ipad-launch.md) |
| April 10, 2010 | Smolensk air disaster — a Polish Air Force Tu-154 crashes in dense fog near Smolensk, killing all 96 aboard, including President Lech Kaczyński, en route to a Katyn massacre commemoration | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/smolensk-air-disaster.md) |
| April 14, 2010 | Eyjafjallajökull eruption — Iceland's Eyjafjallajökull volcano erupts, closing most European airspace and cancelling roughly 104,000 flights over six days | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/eyjafjallajokull-eruption.md) |
| April 20, 2010 | Deepwater Horizon explosion — Blowout and explosion on BP's Deepwater Horizon rig kills 11 workers, beginning the largest marine oil spill in history | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/deepwater-horizon-oil-spill.md) |
| May 1 – October 31, 2010 | Shanghai Expo 2010 opens — China hosts its first World's Fair under the theme "Better City, Better Life," drawing a record 73.08 million visits over 184 days, the highest attendance in Expo history | Society & Economics | [details](society-economics/shanghai-expo-2010.md) |
| May 2, 2010 | European sovereign debt crisis — euro-area states and the IMF agree a €110 billion bailout for Greece, then a €750 billion eurozone backstop on May 9–10, opening the acute phase of the crisis | Society & Economics | [details](society-economics/european-sovereign-debt-crisis.md) |
| May 10, 2010 | 2010 Philippine presidential election — Benigno Aquino III wins on the strength of his mother Corazon Aquino's People Power legacy | People | [details](people/corazon-aquino.md) |
| May 17, 2010 | *Biutiful* premieres at Cannes — Alejandro González Iñárritu's Spanish-language drama debuts in competition; Javier Bardem shares the Best Actor prize and later earns the first Oscar Best Actor nomination for an all-Spanish-language role | Arts & Culture | [details](arts-culture/biutiful.md) |
| May 19, 2010 | 2010 Thai political crisis — Military crackdown ends a two-month Red Shirt occupation of Bangkok, killing at least 91 people and wounding over 1,800 | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/2010-thai-political-crisis.md) |
| May 20, 2010 | First self-replicating synthetic cell — the J. Craig Venter Institute announces JCVI-syn1.0, a bacterium controlled by a chemically synthesized 1.08-million-base-pair genome | Science & Technology | [details](science-technology/synthetic-cell-jcvi-syn1.md) |
| May 20, 2010 | J. Craig Venter and synthetic biology leadership — geneticist J. Craig Venter leads the JCVI team publishing JCVI-syn1.0 in *Science*, framing biology as an engineering discipline and triggering an international bioethics debate | Science & Technology | [details](science-technology/craig-venter-synthetic-biology.md) |
| June 11 – July 11, 2010 | FIFA World Cup in South Africa — First World Cup held on the African continent; Spain wins their first title | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/fifa-world-cup-2010.md) |
| Throughout 2010 | Global jobless recovery — world output rebounds from the 2008–09 crisis but unemployment stays persistently high (US 9.6%, eurozone 10.1%, ILO ~205 million jobless), defining a "jobless recovery" | Society & Economics | [details](society-economics/global-unemployment-and-economic-recovery-2010.md) |
| June 17, 2010 | Stuxnet identified — VirusBlokAda isolates the Stuxnet worm, later revealed as the first known cyberweapon to physically sabotage industrial equipment at Iran's Natanz facility | Science & Technology | [details](science-technology/stuxnet.md) |
| June 18, 2010 | *Toy Story 3* released — Pixar's animated sequel opens, grossing $1.067 billion worldwide to become the highest-grossing film of 2010 and the first animated feature to surpass $1 billion | Arts & Culture | [details](arts-culture/toy-story-3.md) |
| June 24, 2010 | iPhone 4 launched — Apple releases the iPhone 4 with its 326 ppi Retina display, glass-and-steel design, front-facing camera, and FaceTime, selling 1.7 million units in the first weekend | Science & Technology | [details](science-technology/iphone-4-launch.md) |
| July 8, 2010 | LeBron James' "The Decision" — NBA star LeBron James announces on a live ESPN special that he will sign with the Miami Heat, inaugurating the league's modern player-empowerment superteam era | People | [details](people/lebron-james-the-decision.md) |
| July 15, 2010 | Deepwater Horizon well temporarily capped — After 87 days, a temporary cap stops the flow from the Macondo well | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/deepwater-horizon-oil-spill.md) |
| July 16, 2010 | *Inception* released — Christopher Nolan's science-fiction thriller opens, grossing $839 million worldwide and winning 4 Academy Awards | Arts & Culture | [details](arts-culture/inception.md) |
| July 21, 2010 | Dodd-Frank Act signed — Most sweeping US financial regulation reforms since the Great Depression enacted in response to the 2008 financial crisis | Society & Economics | [details](society-economics/dodd-frank-act.md) |
| July 27, 2010 | Pakistan floods begin — Record monsoon rainfall triggers catastrophic flooding that eventually affects ~20 million people across one-fifth of Pakistan | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/pakistan-floods.md) |
| August 31, 2010 | Kepler-9 multi-planet system confirmed — NASA confirms Kepler-9b and Kepler-9c, the first multiple planets observed transiting a single sun-like star, via transit timing variations | Science & Technology | [details](science-technology/kepler-9-exoplanet-discovery.md) |
| September 6, 2010 | *The King's Speech* premiered — Tom Hooper's historical drama premieres at the Telluride Film Festival; it would win four Academy Awards and become the most successful independent British film ever released | Arts & Culture | [details](arts-culture/the-kings-speech.md) |
| September 19, 2010 | Deepwater Horizon well permanently sealed — Macondo well permanently sealed via a relief-well "bottom kill," 152 days after the April 20 blowout | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/deepwater-horizon-macondo-well-capped.md) |
| September 29, 2010 | Gliese 581g discovery announced — Astronomers announce a candidate habitable-zone exoplanet 20.3 light-years away; the widely publicized claim is later disputed and reclassified as unconfirmed | Science & Technology | [details](science-technology/gliese-581g-exoplanet.md) |
| October 1, 2010 | *The Social Network* released — David Fincher's film about Facebook's founding opens; wins 3 Academy Awards and grosses $225 million | Arts & Culture | [details](arts-culture/the-social-network.md) |
| October 6, 2010 | Instagram launched — Photo-sharing app launches on iOS, reaching 1 million users within roughly two months and pioneering the mobile-first social media era | Science & Technology | [details](science-technology/instagram-launch.md) |
| October 13, 2010 | Chilean mining rescue — All 33 trapped miners rescued alive after 69 days underground at the San José Mine | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/chilean-mining-rescue.md) |
| November 2, 2010 | 2010 US midterm elections — Republicans gain 63 House seats, the largest swing since 1948, retaking the chamber; the Tea Party movement reshapes the decade's politics | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/2010-us-midterm-elections.md) |
| November 4, 2010 | Microsoft Kinect launched — Microsoft releases the controller-free Kinect motion sensor for Xbox 360, which sets a Guinness record as the fastest-selling consumer electronics device (8 million units in 60 days) | Science & Technology | [details](science-technology/kinect-launch.md) |
| November 13, 2010 | Aung San Suu Kyi released from house arrest — Myanmar's pro-democracy leader freed after roughly 15 years of detention, days after the junta's first multiparty elections in 20 years | People | [details](people/aung-san-suu-kyi.md) |
| November 28, 2010 | WikiLeaks releases diplomatic cables — WikiLeaks publishes 251,287 classified US State Department cables in major intelligence disclosure | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/wikileaks-cables.md) |
| November 28, 2010 | Julian Assange and WikiLeaks — the Cablegate release brings WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange to global prominence; he surrenders to London police on December 7, opening a decade-long legal battle | People | [details](people/julian-assange.md) |
| December 17, 2010 | Arab Spring begins — Tunisia's Jasmine Revolution begins, setting off a wave of uprisings across the Arab world | History & Politics | [details](history-politics/arab-spring-tunisia.md) |
| December 17, 2010 | Mohamed Bouazizi self-immolation — Tunisian street vendor's protest in Sidi Bouzid becomes the catalyst of the Arab Spring | People | [details](people/mohamed-bouazizi.md) |

<!-- END GENERATED: timeline -->
